metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `execution_mode: 1-TURN` in the prompt front-matter spec with `execution_mode: single_turn` — the only valid single-turn value in the closed `EXECUTION_MODES` enum (`src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompts.py:74`).
findings.finding: The plan specifies the prompt's front-matter field as `execution_mode: 1-TURN`, but the codebase defines a closed enum `EXECUTION_MODES = ("single_turn", "agentic")` in `src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompts.py` line 74. The value `1-TURN` is not a member of this enum. `get_prompt()` validates against this enum and raises `PromptError` for any unrecognized value, so authoring the enrich prompt exactly as specified will cause a hard failure at load time.
findings.location: What / Scope sections — 'Prompt in the prompt library … with front-matter `execution_mode: 1-TURN`'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan's prompt front-matter spec from `execution_mode: 1-TURN` to `execution_mode: single_turn` to match the closed enum `EXECUTION_MODES = ("single_turn", "agentic")` enforced by `get_prompt()` in `src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompts.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `execution_mode: 1-TURN` in the prompt front-matter spec with the valid value `execution_mode: single_turn` (the only accepted single-call mode in `EXECUTION_MODES`).
findings.finding: The plan specifies prompt front-matter `execution_mode: 1-TURN`, but the codebase enforces a closed enum `EXECUTION_MODES = ('single_turn', 'agentic')` validated at parse time in `src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompts.py:232`. A prompt file declaring `1-TURN` would raise `PromptError` on first use.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Prompt in the prompt library … with front-matter `execution_mode: 1-TURN`'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the front-matter line to `execution_mode: single_turn`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `execution_mode: 1-TURN` in the plan's prompt front-matter spec with `execution_mode: single_turn` — the only valid single-call value in `EXECUTION_MODES` (src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompts.py:74).
findings.finding: The plan specifies `execution_mode: 1-TURN` in the prompt front-matter, but the framework's closed enum only accepts `single_turn` or `agentic`. Loading the prompt with `1-TURN` will raise a PromptError at runtime.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Prompt in the prompt library … with front-matter `execution_mode: 1-TURN`'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Scope bullet to: 'Prompt … with front-matter `execution_mode: single_turn`' (matching the existing single-call prompts such as `plan_review_COH.md`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit instruction to register a `ticket_digest` Pydantic response model via `register_contract('ticket_digest', ...)` in `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py`, mirroring the `completion_verdict` pattern, so `response_model_for` resolves the correct model instead of falling back to `findings_response_model`.
findings.finding: The plan directs the implementer to register the output schema 'under `src/rebar/schemas/`' and validate via `structured.py`, but the actual pipeline for structured LLM operations binds the Pydantic response model through `contracts.py`'s `_CONTRACTS` registry (`register_contract(name, builder)`). No plan instruction covers this registration step, so `response_model_for('ticket_digest')` would fall back to the findings model, silently mismapping the digest output.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Output schema registered under `src/rebar/schemas/` and validated via `structured.py`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the Scope/AC inconsistency: change the Scope to say 'library entry in `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py`' (consistent with AC-1's `rebar.llm.enrich` target and the established pattern for all other LLM ops).
findings.finding: The Scope section says 'library entry in `src/rebar/__init__.py`' but the AC says `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)`. If implemented literally, adding an LLM op to the top-level `rebar` namespace creates a new surface inconsistent with the established pattern where ALL LLM operations (review_ticket, verify_completion, review_code, scan_epics_for_spec, review_plan) are exported exclusively from `rebar.llm.__init__` — never from `rebar.__init__`.
findings.location: Scope section ('library entry in `src/rebar/__init__.py`') vs AC line 1 ('rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet, replace 'library entry in `src/rebar/__init__.py`' with 'exported from `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py`', matching how review_ticket, verify_completion, scan_epics_for_spec, and review_plan are already registered.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the library-entry target from `src/rebar/__init__.py` to `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py` to match where all other LLM ops (`review_ticket`, `verify_completion`, etc.) are exported, and to satisfy the `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` AC.
findings.finding: The plan says the new op should get a 'library entry in `src/rebar/__init__.py`', but `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` is an LLM operation — the existing peer operations (`review_ticket`, `verify_completion`, etc.) are exported from `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py`, NOT from `src/rebar/__init__.py`. The top-level `src/rebar/__init__.py` has no LLM imports; adding the enrich op there would break the package's layering (rebar core importing from rebar.llm). The correct file for the library entry is `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py`.
findings.location: Scope section — 'New op module (e.g. src/rebar/llm/enrich.py) + library entry in src/rebar/__init__.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the Scope bullet to: 'New op module `src/rebar/llm/enrich.py` + export in `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py` (alongside `review_ticket`, `verify_completion`, etc.)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the library-entry target from `src/rebar/__init__.py` to `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py` (where all other LLM op exports live), consistent with the AC's `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` call shape.
findings.finding: The plan directs adding a 'library entry in `src/rebar/__init__.py`', but the AC specifies `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` — and all existing LLM operations (review_ticket, verify_completion, review_plan, review_code) are exported from `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py`, not from `src/rebar/__init__.py`. The store-facade `src/rebar/__init__.py` contains no LLM ops. The named edit target is the wrong file.
findings.location: Scope section: 'New op module … + library entry in `src/rebar/__init__.py`'; AC line 1: '`rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)`'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, change 'library entry in `src/rebar/__init__.py`' to 'library entry in `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the Cupid pattern decision: why LLM-as-feature-extractor instead of embeddings, the digest schema rationale, and the expected dedup strategy downstream.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern (Cupid LLM feature extractor as a dedup strategy without embeddings) and adds a new pipeline stage, but does not call for an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry to capture the decision rationale.
findings.location: ## Why / ## Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Create docs/adr/0001-cupid-digest-extractor.md (or add to CLAUDE.md) capturing: (1) problem — paraphrase detection without vector DB; (2) decision — LLM one-time extraction to normalized digest; (3) consequences — schema evolution tied to S5/S6 retrieval design; (4) alternatives considered.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the canonical prompt location (remove the 'or' and pick one directory) so the executor does not have to choose.
findings.finding: The prompt file location is given as `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/` OR `.rebar/prompts/` with no decision rule, deferring the choice to the executor.
findings.location: Scope section, bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: If an existing op's prompt already lives in one of these directories, match it (e.g., 'Prompt in `.rebar/prompts/` consistent with existing op prompts').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make AC5 measurable by stating the minimum fixture count and a concrete per-field assertion rule (e.g., 'At least 3 fixture tickets; each digest must have ≥1 proposition, ≥1 problem_keyword, and a non-empty component_or_area; epic-sized body (~4000 tokens) must produce ≤6 propositions').
findings.finding: AC5's success condition 'assert extraction captures the core problem/entities on representative tickets' is subjective: 'captures the core problem' has no threshold, no fixture count, and no definition of pass/fail, making it unmeasurable without further specification.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that AC5 eval fixtures use independently human-verified expected outputs (not LLM-snapshot-recorded values), with explicit pass/fail criteria for each digest field rather than full-object equality against recorded outputs.
findings.finding: The eval fixtures described in AC5 risk the self-authored-oracle / tautology anti-pattern: if the developer both authors the prompt and writes 'expected' digest outputs by running the prompt and recording its output, the fixture locks in whatever the LLM happened to produce rather than independently verified ground-truth quality. This is MAJOR per E5 (change-detector/tautology = MAJOR).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5: 'Eval fixtures assert extraction captures the core problem/entities on representative tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note that fixture 'expected' digests are authored by a human reviewer who read the source ticket text and determined the correct problem_keywords, component_or_area, key_entities, and propositions independently of running the prompt — or alternatively, specify semantic/substring assertions (e.g. 'propositions must contain X') rather than full-object snapshot equality.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or eval fixture that covers schema-validation-failure: when the LLM returns a structurally noncompliant response, the op raises a documented exception type (not an unhandled internal error) so callers can handle it the same way as AC4's disabled-LLM path.
findings.finding: The plan covers the disabled/absent-LLM failure path (AC4) but does not address what happens when the LLM responds but returns malformed JSON or a schema-noncompliant structured output — a distinct and likely failure mode for the structured-mode op.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 and the schema validation reference in 'Output schema registered under src/rebar/schemas/ and validated via structured.py'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4 or as AC4b: 'When structured output fails schema validation (missing/wrong-type field), the op raises [SpecificException] with a message identifying the field — callers treat this identically to the absent-LLM path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the proposition count range (currently '2-6') a named constant or prompt variable (e.g., MIN_PROPOSITIONS / MAX_PROPOSITIONS) so it can be tuned from a single location as downstream dedup experiments inform the optimal value.
findings.finding: The "2-6 atomic propositions" count constraint is a threshold asserted in the schema/prompt that will need tuning as downstream dedup is built, but is not represented as a configurable parameter.
findings.location: ## What — "2-6 atomic problem/repro propositions"

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the signature ambiguity in AC1: state whether `enrich()` accepts raw ticket text (e.g., `enrich(ticket_text: str)`) or a ticket_id with a thin fetch stub, and if the latter, name the fetch interface it calls so the executor does not have to invent it.
findings.finding: The public signature `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` implies the function fetches ticket content by ID, but ticket storage/fetch (S2) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE. An executor cannot implement this without deciding whether the callable receives a ticket_id (requiring a fetch dependency that is out of scope) or the raw ticket text — this choice blocks implementation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 / Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` with `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_text: str) -> DigestSchema` in AC1 (the internal callable can accept text directly, and the ticket_id→text lookup is S2's concern). Mention that callers are responsible for supplying text, keeping S1 storage-free.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC (e.g., `pytest src/rebar/tests/test_enrich.py -v` for AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5; a specific invocation showing the raised exception for AC4) so that done-ness is deterministically verifiable.
findings.finding: No proving command is specified for any of the five acceptance criteria. None of the ACs include a `pytest` invocation, CLI call, or other deterministic command that would produce evidence of completion — every criterion is written as a property assertion ('returns', 'are excluded', 'yields identical', 'raises/reports cleanly') without a verification path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five bullets)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) that explicitly exercises the live Anthropic path end-to-end — e.g., 'A manual or CI integration test calls `enrich()` against the real Anthropic API with a representative ticket and the returned digest validates against the schema' — or document a rationale for deferring this to a named child ticket.
findings.finding: AC1 requires `rebar.llm.enrich()` to return a validated digest but no AC exercises the live Anthropic API end-to-end. The eval fixtures (AC5) could pass entirely on mocked or cached responses, meaning the Anthropic integration path is never proven to run in production.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1, AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the `enrich(ticket_id)` signature in AC1 with the out-of-scope storage constraint: either change the public API to `enrich(ticket_text: str)` (or `enrich(ticket_id, ticket_text)`), or explicitly add ticket-text retrieval as in-scope with a defined source.
findings.finding: AC1 specifies the public interface as `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` (takes a ticket ID), but the What section says the op 'reads a ticket's full text', and the Scope explicitly excludes storage (S2) — leaving it unspecified how the function resolves a `ticket_id` to text without a storage layer in scope.
findings.location: AC1 (`rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)`) vs What section ('reads a ticket's full text') vs Scope ('OUT of scope: storage (S2)')
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC1 to `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_text: str) -> Digest` (accepting pre-fetched text), or add an overload `enrich(ticket_id=None, ticket_text=None)` and note that the caller is responsible for text retrieval, keeping storage out of scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add (or extend AC1 to cover) explicit verification of the prompt front-matter (`execution_mode: 1-TURN`) and the public import path (`from rebar import enrich` or equivalent), so these scoped deliverables have traceable acceptance criteria.
findings.finding: Two scoped deliverables — (a) the prompt file with `execution_mode: 1-TURN` front-matter and (b) the `__init__.py` library entry — have no dedicated acceptance criterion that would verify their existence or correctness independently of an end-to-end run.
findings.location: Scope section (prompt + library entry deliverables) vs Acceptance Criteria (AC1–AC5)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 or add AC6: 'The prompt file includes `execution_mode: 1-TURN` front-matter and `rebar.llm.enrich` is importable from the top-level `rebar` package.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/backoff behavior for transient LLM errors (rate limits, network failures) and a configurable request timeout, OR explicitly confirm the existing completion.py scaffolding already covers these and cite the mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan specifies graceful handling for a disabled/absent LLM but does not address retry/backoff for transient failures (rate limits, network errors) or timeouts for slow Anthropic responses — leaving callers potentially blocked indefinitely on a degraded API.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC 4: "Disabled/absent-LLM path raises/reports cleanly"

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: the enrichment op must emit at minimum a structured log entry per call (ticket_id, success/failure, latency_ms, token_count) and, optionally, a metric counter for failures/skips.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new external-call failure point (LLM enrichment op) but does not include observability requirements — no mention of logging, metrics, or tracing for enrichment latency, errors, or skipped tickets.
findings.location: ## Scope / ## Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC1 and AC3: clarify whether `rebar.llm.enrich` accepts raw ticket text (idempotency trivially satisfied) or a ticket_id with an internal fetch, and if the latter, specify that idempotency is per ticket snapshot/version, not per ID.
findings.finding: The public API signature `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` (AC1) conflicts with the idempotency guarantee in AC3, which is defined over 'identical input text'. If the function receives a ticket_id and fetches ticket text internally, idempotency depends on the ticket's content not changing — but the plan never says the op pins or accepts text directly. The two ACs are in tension: one anchors the contract to an ID, the other to raw text.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 (`rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)`) and AC3 ('identical input text yields an identical digest payload')
findings.suggested_fix: Change the public signature to `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_text: str) -> DigestSchema` (with a thin `enrich_by_id` wrapper that fetches and delegates), so AC3's 'identical input text' framing directly matches the primary callable's contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the Scope section whether CLI (`_cli/_llm_commands.py`) and MCP (`_mcp_llm.py`) wiring for `enrich` is in or out of scope for this stage.
findings.finding: The plan names no consumers outside `src/rebar/llm/` that need updating: the CLI (`src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py`) and MCP server (`src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py`) are the standard integration points for every LLM op but are not mentioned. If intentionally deferred to a later stage this should be explicitly acknowledged; as written, a reader cannot tell whether wiring is in or out of scope.
findings.location: Scope section: 'IN scope / OUT of scope' list

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `execution_mode: 1-TURN` in the prompt front-matter spec with `execution_mode: single_turn` — the only valid single-call value in the closed enum `EXECUTION_MODES = ('single_turn', 'agentic')` (prompts.py).
findings.finding: The plan specifies `execution_mode: 1-TURN` in the prompt front-matter, but `1-TURN` is not a valid prompt execution_mode value in the codebase. The closed enum is `('single_turn', 'agentic')` (prompts.py EXECUTION_MODES); `1-TURN` is a plan-review criteria routing tier token (criteria_routing.json, registry.py) and has no meaning in prompt front-matter. Using it would either be silently ignored or cause a loading/validation error, leaving the enrich prompt running in the wrong mode.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Prompt in the prompt library … with front-matter `execution_mode: 1-TURN`'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the front-matter line to `execution_mode: single_turn` (matching every other single-call prompt in the catalog, e.g. `plan_review_T2.md`, `plan_review_hedge.md`, `code-review-coach.md`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the prompt front-matter value from `execution_mode: 1-TURN` to `execution_mode: single_turn` to match the only valid enum values (`single_turn` | `agentic`) recognized by `EXECUTION_MODES` in `prompts.py` and the runner dispatch in `runs.py`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `execution_mode: 1-TURN` in the prompt front-matter, but the codebase's closed enum is `EXECUTION_MODES = ('single_turn', 'agentic')` — '1-TURN' is not a valid value and will silently fall through to `agentic` (tool-using loop) behavior instead of the intended single-call extraction.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Prompt in the prompt library … with front-matter `execution_mode: 1-TURN`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `execution_mode: 1-TURN` with `execution_mode: single_turn` in the prompt front-matter spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan (a) that the disabled/absent-LLM path raises `LLMUnavailableError` (the established contract all callers already catch), and (b) that the Pydantic response model for the digest enforces the '2-6 propositions' minimum-length constraint as a `field_validator`, co-located with the contract definition (matching the `structured.py` pattern of 'bounds in validators, not the JSON Schema').
findings.finding: AC4 specifies 'Disabled/absent-LLM path raises/reports cleanly so callers can gracefully skip' but does not name which error type from the established hierarchy (`LLMUnavailableError`, `LLMConfigError`, `StructuredOutputError`) the op should raise or re-raise, nor does it specify the decision rule for a partial-but-schema-valid digest (e.g., `propositions=[]`). The processing protocol co-location requirement (T8-b) is unmet: the schema defines minimum cardinality ('2-6 atomic propositions') in prose but no co-located validation rule or fallback.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4; Digest shape description: '2-6 atomic problem/repro propositions'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the negative 'logs/stack-traces/code explicitly discarded' exclusion instruction in the prompt spec with an affirmative constraint on the `propositions[]` field (e.g., 'each proposition must be a natural-language problem statement, ≤ 25 words'), letting the schema enforce the shape rather than naming the excluded content.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the prompt to exclude 'logs, stack traces, and code explicitly discarded' — a classic pink-elephant / negative-instruction antipattern. Naming the items to exclude primes the model to process and potentially surface them. The established rebar pattern (e.g., `completion_verifier.md`, `plan_review_completion.md`) uses affirmative instructions about what TO emit, not what to suppress.
findings.location: What section: 'logs, stack traces, and code explicitly discarded'
findings.suggested_fix: In the prompt body, replace any 'do not include X' clauses with a positive field-level constraint: 'Each proposition: a concise natural-language statement of a distinct problem symptom or reproduction step (no code, no raw log lines; ≤ 25 words).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
